label: Suture removal or wound check without infection concern
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician-documented wound check, suture removal, or staple removal plan.
Clinician documents wound is healing as expected or ready for removal.
No infection, dehiscence, retained foreign body, tendon/nerve/vascular risk, bite wound, open fracture, or specialist-directed wound plan.
exclusion_criteria: Wound infection concern, pus, spreading redness, fever, red streaks, or systemic illness.

Wound dehiscence, wound not ready for removal, early suture/staple loss, retained foreign body, tendon/nerve/vascular concern, open fracture, joint violation, bite wound, dirty wound, or poor follow-up.
Pediatric pathway, immunocompromised host, diabetic foot wound, anticoagulation-related bleeding concern, or specialist-directed wound plan.
must_not_miss: Tendon, nerve, vascular, or joint injury.
default_follow_up: Follow up on the wound schedule your clinician gave you.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports suture/staple wound care, infection warning signs, and following clinician instructions for wound care and removal timing. It does not support autonomous ED decisions about whether a wound is ready for removal, so runtime requires clinician-documented readiness and no infection/dehiscence concern.

terms: no dehiscence infection foreign body tendon nerve vascular concern

review.notes: Phase 184 retired this draft to product-layer fallback because suture removal readiness, timing by body site, cosmesis, and specialist-directed wound plans require explicit clinician approval before ontology mode.
